name: ted@tedcruz.org
subject: Did you hear what Rush said?
disclaimer_text: paid for by the same group who failed to defeat us in Iowa
body:
CRUZ FOR PRESIDENT

Friend, I'm truly humbled. Rush Limbaugh just defended our
campaign against the unrelenting attacks... even going as far as
comparing me to Ronald Reagan.

>From the Desk of:
TED CRUZ

Friend, I'm truly humbled.

Rush Limbaugh just defended our campaign against the unrelenting
attacks... even going as far as comparing me to Ronald Reagan.

Here is what he said:

"...if conservatism is the dominating factor in how you vote,
there is no other choice for you in this campaign than Ted Cruz
... This is the closest in our lifetimes we have ever been to
Ronald Reagan."

While Rush reaches millions of conservatives every day -- and I
can't thank him enough for his gracious comments -- it is your
continued support I desperately need today.

You may not be able to go on national radio and defend my
candidacy or vouch for my conservative credentials... but you can
do something far greater for me right now from anywhere in the
country.

I'm sure you know by now that this is increasingly looking like a
two person race -- me vs. Donald Trump.

And while Donald Trump is a well-connected multi-billionaire -- I
rely on smaller, individual contributions.

That's why your support is so critical. Will you click here to
make an emergency gift of $35, $50, or $100 to my campaign?

Because as of this moment, I am still $56,031 short of meeting my
South Carolina budget to respond to Donald Trump's nasty attacks
and the more than $1.5 million in attack ads airing against me --
paid for by the same group who failed to defeat us in Iowa.

Rush's defense of me against Donald Trump and the mainstream
media was huge, but it won't matter if the right people don't see
it!

Will you help me get Rush's comments in front of millions of
Republican primary voters when it counts most -- just days before
primary votes are cast -- by making an URGENT contribution today?

Friend, I'm about to step on the debate stage tonight to defend
our conservative ideals.

Now, I'm counting on you to do your part.

CLICK HERE TO CHIP IN $35, $50, $100, OR $250 >>>

I truly cannot afford to come up short.

With the voting in South Carolina just a week away, time is of
the essence. That is why I'm emailing you now.

Here's the bottom line: we won Iowa and we're building real
momentum in key states. With your immediate help we will win this
nomination, defeat the Washington Cartel, and reignite liberty
across America.

But I can't do it alone.

CLICK HERE TO CHIP IN $35, $50, $100, OR $250 >>>
I'm personally asking, will you to chip in $35, $50, or $250
right now BEFORE we run out of time?

Rush had my back. Will you?

For liberty,

Ted Cruz

PAID FOR BY CRUZ
FOR PRESIDENTwww.tedcruz.org

subject: I've been preparing
body:

That's why I'm working hard and need your support.

[**Friend, I'm asking you to chip in $1 to show you have my back.**](http://click1.email.jeb2016.com/fgqztbylrnjwqpqdwbfzrwcpyywclptlzbgbjlpbnnjq_ufpnnmcy.html)

name: Murray Carson
subject: My dad needs to hear from us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carson America, Inc
body:
---
| | [](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33531027:sUvDKLCmN:m:1:876315805:8885F9D3FE58CB9D63214A5B6FFEF553:r)
---

Peter,

Tonight at 9pm ET in South Carolina, the 6 remaining candidates will take the stage for the next Republican Debate on CBS.

**Thanks to you and millions of other Americans, my dad will be one of them.
**
It makes me proud because every time he steps onto that stage, millions of Americans get the chance to learn more about the man I feel blessed to call my father -- a man of faith, conviction and calm, quiet strength who wants nothing more than to heal this great country.

But if we are ever to replace the establishment and self-interested politicians, we must have a big night tonight, and a big day on February 20th when the people of South Carolina head to the polls. If we want to win back the country for the American people, for us, we have to continue to work together and support the man who is not in this race for ego, but because he believes we can reclaim the dream of our Founding Fathers: Dr. Ben Carson!

**So for tonight, we've set a simple goal. With 7 days until the South Carolina primary, we're looking for 15,000 supporters to donate $7. That's just 300 people per state.

****[Will you help us hit our goal by donating just $7 or more right now?](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33531027:sUvDKLCmN:m:1:876315805:8885F9D3FE58CB9D63214A5B6FFEF553:r)**

Not only will your donation help to encourage my dad before tonight's debate, but it will directly bolster both our South Carolina advertising budget and our grassroots, Get Out the Vote efforts. Ultimately, this is about reaching as many voters as possible and we can't do it without you.

[**So please, take a moment to make a secure donation of just $7 or more.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33531027:sUvDKLCmN:m:1:876315805:8885F9D3FE58CB9D63214A5B6FFEF553:r)

On behalf of my whole family, thank you for your support. We truly appreciate it, and hope that you'll be tuning into CBS tonight at 9pm ET.

Sincerely,

Murray Carson

| | |

| **Friend** -- Welcome to the first edition of Murphy Miles - the best way for you to track what's happening as Patrick hits the road, meeting people all over Florida.
---
|
### Checked In At
## Pompano Beach with the hardworking men and women of LiUNA!
Patrick was incredibly proud to pick up the endorsement of the Laborers' International Union of North America (LiUNA): |
---
| |
|
### Along For The Ride
## Senators Cory Booker & Claire McCaskill
In addition to the endorsement of LiUNA, Patrick recently picked up the endorsements of Senator Cory Booker (D-NJ) and Senator Claire McCaskill (D-MO). They know that Patrick's race is one of the closest in the country -- and that it could determine control of the Senate. This is a tough race -- and we need all hands on deck. Are you in?
[Click here to give Patrick your endorsement.](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae22c6/738133e4/34de0bf2/7f83d21b/1812339093/VEsH/ "http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/e160212")
|
### Shifting Gears
## Campaign Finance
At the LiUNA event, I spoke to some great, hard-working Floridians. Many of them had worked construction all their lives, and supported their families on that salary. They are worried about things like paying healthcare bills, sending their small kids to college one day, and saving up for retirement. Hard-working Floridians don't have millions of dollars to run ads about health care, or keeping college tuition down, or Social Security and Medicare. They aren't the Koch brothers. But that doesn't mean their voices shouldn't matter -- in fact, they should matter more. |
### POLL
Do you support campaign finance reform to reverse _Citizens United?_ | [Yes](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae22c6/738133e4/34de0bf2/7f83d214/1812339093/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160212y?refcode=pme160212y_m&recurring=true&amount=25.00") | [No](http://fl.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae22c6/738133e4/34de0bf2/7f83d215/1812339093/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160212n?refcode=pme160212n_m&recurring=true&amount=25.00")

_Citizens United_ is one of the biggest threats to our democracy. Right-wing special interest groups shouldn't be able to buy our elections.
I've been fighting _Citizens United_ since the moment the decision was handed down. And I will continue until the disastrous effects of this decision are no more.

\-- Patrick

subject: Cup of Joe: February 13, 2016
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Joe Sestak
body:
www.JoeSestak.com
CUP OF JOE

February 13th, 2016
Weekly Newsletter

Good morning! I wanted to provide an update about what I've been up to this past week. Thanks so much for reading. I appreciate your support.

Walking with Sestak: 'Joe marches to his own drum' in Senate race

by Wallace McKelvey

SKIPPACK -- There's no tougher audience than a roomful of incarcerated veterans who've endured two hours of bloviating academics, judges and wardens.

By the time Joe Sestak stood to speak, the wooden pews of the chapel at Graterford Prison had thinned. The lunch call came a half hour earlier and the 70 or so men who remained were sacrificing cafeteria privileges.

The retired Navy admiral sat alongside them through the Veterans Day speeches, occasionally leaning in to commiserate with the men beside him in brown D.O.C uniforms. When it was finally his turn, Sestak bolted up and politely waved off the microphone and the podium.

"I thank you because that's more than this country has ever done for you," Sestak said, in a near-shout over the din of floor and wall fans recirculating the chapel's stale, hot air.

Some of the men, particularly the older ones, nodded along as the onetime U.S. congressman paced the aisle in jeans and a fatigue-green flight jacket emblazoned with the George Washington Battle Group he commanded during the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq. His hands, like his feet, kept moving, adding kinetic energy to his words about drug sentencing, prison reform and PTSD.

Most of the men already knew about Sestak's 2016 run for the U.S. Senate, but the candidate failed to mention it in this particular stump speech. Afterward, he quipped amiably: "It wouldn't have helped much here."

Sestak has visited a prison every Veterans Day for nearly a decade, even after losing to the now-incumbent Sen. Pat Toomey in the Republican wave election of 2010. And each year, the men continue to send him a formal invitation.

'Amongst the crowd'

Sestak would seem like an ideal candidate for either party: A decorated Navy officer and Harvard graduate who grew up in Philadelphia's blue-collar suburbs, the eldest son in a Catholic family with seven siblings.

During his four years in Congress, from 2007 to 2011, Sestak was known for his constituent services -- his office handled some 18,000 cases -- and for working across party lines on everything from autism research to small business tax credits.

At virtually every campaign stop, whether it's room of prisoners or a handful of Harrisburgers in a cramped community center, he's fond of clasping his hands together as he expounds upon the importance of "rugged individualism and common enterprise" working in tandem.
"I don't want them to think I've forgotten them," Sestak said

CLICK HERE TO READ MORE FROM THE PATRIOT NEWS ARTICLE:

http://www.pennlive.com/news/2016/02/joe_sestak_senate_walk.html

Visiting a VFW in York County

I was honored to join fellow veterans at the VFW Post in Spring Grove to lead a conversation about policies to expand employment and business ownership opportunities for veterans, improve veteran health care, and combat veteran homelessness.

When a service member tells his brother-in-arms that he "has their six," their performance backs up that promise with more than just words. Our veterans, who have served and sacrificed so that we can live freely, deserve to come home to a nation where we all have their six.

Those We Served

When Brian Hammock was honorably discharged from the Air Force after more than 9 years of service to our nation, he found himself without the resources or support to help him transition into civilian life. When he came to me for help after agencies and others could not, I had his back.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Tkn5G9ZgZRo

Meeting with Editorial Boards to Talk about Policies to Help Pennsylvanians

I stopped by the York Daily Record and also the York Dispatch to talk about the policies in my Plan to Restor e the American Dream for PA Working Families, what I learned while walking across the state with regular Pennsylvanians, as well as the nat ional security challenges that we face.

Please click one of the videos to the left -- or both -- to hear what we discussed!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n58WXxb9GO8

http://www.yorkdispatch.com/story/news/politics/elections/2016/02/10/senate-candidate-sestak-stops-york-dispatch/80184180/

Meet "Herman from Mount Vernon"

I took Herman the duck -- or "Herman from Mount Vernon" -- everywhere with me when I was embarked on the USS GEORGE WASHINGTON aircraft carrier because I wanted my then one-year-old daughter Alex to know I was thinking about her every day, and she would know later through Herman's eyes what we were doing during our deployment to Afghanistan and Iraq. clean_body: www.JoeSestak.com CUP OF JOE February 13th, 2016 Weekly Newsletter Good morning! I wanted to provide an update about what I've been up to this past week. Thanks so much for reading. I appreciate your support. Walking with Sestak: 'Joe marches to his own drum' in Senate race by Wallace McKelvey SKIPPACK -- There's no tougher audience than a roomful of incarcerated veterans who've endured two hours of bloviating academics, judges and wardens. By the time Joe Sestak stood to speak, the wooden pews of the chapel at Graterford Prison had thinned. The lunch call came a half hour earlier and the 70 or so men who remained were sacrificing cafeteria privileges. The retired Navy admiral sat alongside them through the Veterans Day speeches, occasionally leaning in to commiserate with the men beside him in brown D.O.C uniforms. When it was finally his turn, Sestak bolted up and politely waved off the microphone and the podium. "I thank you because that's more than this country has ever done for you," Sestak said, in a near-shout over the din of floor and wall fans recirculating the chapel's stale, hot air. Some of the men, particularly the older ones, nodded along as the onetime U.S. congressman paced the aisle in jeans and a fatigue-green flight jacket emblazoned with the George Washington Battle Group he commanded during the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq. His hands, like his feet, kept moving, adding kinetic energy to his words about drug sentencing, prison reform and PTSD. Most of the men already knew about Sestak's 2016 run for the U.S. Senate, but the candidate failed to mention it in this particular stump speech. Afterward, he quipped amiably: "It wouldn't have helped much here." Sestak has visited a prison every Veterans Day for nearly a decade, even after losing to the now-incumbent Sen. Pat Toomey in the Republican wave election of 2010. And each year, the men continue to send him a formal invitation. 'Amongst the crowd' Sestak would seem like an ideal candidate for either party: A decorated Navy officer and Harvard graduate who grew up in Philadelphia's blue-collar suburbs, the eldest son in a Catholic family with seven siblings. During his four years in Congress, from 2007 to 2011, Sestak was known for his constituent services -- his office handled some 18,000 cases -- and for working across party lines on everything from autism research to small business tax credits. At virtually every campaign stop, whether it's room of prisoners or a handful of Harrisburgers in a cramped community center, he's fond of clasping his hands together as he expounds upon the importance of "rugged individualism and common enterprise" working in tandem. "I don't want them to think I've forgotten them," Sestak said CLICK HERE TO READ MORE FROM THE PATRIOT NEWS ARTICLE: Visiting a VFW in York County I was honored to join fellow veterans at the VFW Post in Spring Grove to lead a conversation about policies to expand employment and business ownership opportunities for veterans, improve veteran health care, and combat veteran homelessness. When a service member tells his brother-in-arms that he "has their six," their performance backs up that promise with more than just words. Our veterans, who have served and sacrificed so that we can live freely, deserve to come home to a nation where we all have their six. Those We Served When Brian Hammock was honorably discharged from the Air Force after more than 9 years of service to our nation, he found himself without the resources or support to help him transition into civilian life. When he came to me for help after agencies and others could not, I had his back. Meeting with Editorial Boards to Talk about Policies to Help Pennsylvanians I stopped by the York Daily Record and also the York Dispatch to talk about the policies in my Plan to Restor e the American Dream for PA Working Families, what I learned while walking across the state with regular Pennsylvanians, as well as the nat ional security challenges that we face. Please click one of the videos to the left -- or both -- to hear what we discussed! Meet "Herman from Mount Vernon" I took Herman the duck -- or "Herman from Mount Vernon" -- everywhere with me when I was embarked on the USS GEORGE WASHINGTON aircraft carrier because I wanted my then one-year-old daughter Alex to know I was thinking about her every day, and she would know later through Herman's eyes what we were doing during our deployment to Afghanistan and Iraq. Click the video below to hear Herman's stories!
